zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton於大約1310年出生在英國蘇格蘭貝維其可爾蘭頓時，他的父親Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland32歲，母親Mrs. Thomas Cockburn31歲。他於1335年與Mariota De Veteri-Ponte結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1370年在他的家鄉享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton于大约1310年出生在英国苏格兰貝維其可爾蘭頓时，他的父亲Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland32岁，母亲Mrs. Thomas Cockburn31岁。他于1335年与Mariota De Veteri-Ponte结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1370年在他的家乡享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cockburn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian) and northern English: habitational name from a place in Berwickshire named Cockburn, from Old English cocc ‘cock, rooster’ (or the related byname Cocca) + burna ‘stream’ (see Bourne ). This surname is traditionally pronounced Coburn.

variants.name: Bourne
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Coburn
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cockburn  , reflecting the pronunciation.
Altered form of German Kobern.

variants.name: Cobourn
meanings: Scottish and English (Staffordshire): variant of   Cockburn  .

variants.name: Cogburn
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Cockburn  .

nameMeanings.name: Der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Cockburn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian) and northern English: habitational name from a place in Berwickshire named Cockburn, from Old English cocc ‘cock, rooster’ (or the related byname Cocca) + burna ‘stream’ (see Bourne ). This surname is traditionally pronounced Coburn.

variants.name: Bourne
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Coburn
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cockburn  , reflecting the pronunciation.
Altered form of German Kobern.

variants.name: Cobourn
meanings: Scottish and English (Staffordshire): variant of   Cockburn  .

variants.name: Cogburn
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Cockburn  .

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton was born about 1310, in Langton, Berwickshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland, was 32 and his mother, Mrs. Thomas Cockburn, was 31. He married Mariota De Veteri-Ponte in 1335. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died about 1370, in his hometown, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton est né environ 1310, à Langton, Berwickshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Thomas Cockburn, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Mariota De Veteri-Ponte en 1335. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé environ 1370, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton um 1310 in Langton, Berwickshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland 32 und seine Mutter Mrs. Thomas Cockburn 31. Er hat 1335 Mariota De Veteri-Ponte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb um 1370 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

Lui sposato Mariota De Veteri-Ponte nel 1335. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì circa 1370, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Boltonが約1310年にイギリススコットランドバーウィクシュアイアラントンで生まれた時，父Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderlandは32歳，母Mrs. Thomas Cockburnは31歳であった。1335年にMariota De Veteri-Ponteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は約1370年に故郷で62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 스코틀랜드 버윅실르 랭턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Thomas Cockburn(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1335년 Mariota De Veteri-Ponte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1370년 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Alexander De Cockburn Der Cockburn of that ilk and Henderlands, Baron of Langton, Carriden and Bolton родился около 1310 г. в местности лангтон, бирвиккшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Piers Thomas de Cockburn Of Henderland было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Thomas Cockburn, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Mariota De Veteri-Ponte в 1335 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер около 1370 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет.
